description_long: This is a small, single-engine propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the base. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two seats. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel beneath the engine. The exterior is primarily a light gray color with some red accents visible on the nose gear and propeller hub. The overall design suggests a general aviation aircraft suitable for personal or training use.
description: A small, light gray, single-engine propeller airplane with a low-wing design, tricycle landing gear, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, low-mounted wings, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, nose gear, and the symmetrical low-wing span.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail section, wing, and the registration marking on the fuselage.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the cockpit canopy.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted at the nose with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer at the base.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
